topStories.title: Rupee rises for fifth day, rallies to 6-week high
topStories.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened for the fifth consecutive session, closing at 94.33 against the dollar. This gain to a six-week high was supported by Brent crude oil prices falling below $78 per barrel and the unwinding of long dollar positions. The Reserve Bank of India intervened to absorb dollars as the rupee reached an intraday high.

topStories.title: Growth over scale: NSE is priced at a discount to BSE
topStories.summary: NSE's upcoming IPO is set for a discount compared to its peer BSE. This valuation reflects slower growth in NSE's core business. BSE, however, is seeing strong performance. Its derivatives segment has seen significant revenue increases. Transaction income has also surged. This performance is driving BSE's premium valuation in the market.

topStories.title: NSE IPO Bonanza: PSU banks, insurers and early investors eye huge gains
topStories.summary: Dozens of investors, including public sector banks and insurers, are poised for substantial gains from the upcoming National Stock Exchange IPO. SBI alone expects to rake in over ₹5,000 crore. Other funds and institutions, with stakes dating back to the 90s, are also set to receive significant returns.

US stocks: US market's indexes advance with boost from chips, Iran optimism

Oil prices slid as the U.S. and Iran extended an interim agreement, allowing for continued passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

world.title: Apple to raise prices as AI boom pushes up chip costs
world.summary: The firm's outgoing boss Tim Cook did not say when prices would rise or which products would be affected.

business.title: Rupee rises for fifth day, rallies to 6-week high
business.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened for the fifth consecutive session, closing at 94.33 against the dollar. This gain to a six-week high was supported by Brent crude oil prices falling below $78 per barrel and the unwinding of long dollar positions. The Reserve Bank of India intervened to absorb dollars as the rupee reached an intraday high.

business.title: Growth over scale: NSE is priced at a discount to BSE
business.summary: NSE's upcoming IPO is set for a discount compared to its peer BSE. This valuation reflects slower growth in NSE's core business. BSE, however, is seeing strong performance. Its derivatives segment has seen significant revenue increases. Transaction income has also surged. This performance is driving BSE's premium valuation in the market.

business.title: NSE IPO Bonanza: PSU banks, insurers and early investors eye huge gains
business.summary: Dozens of investors, including public sector banks and insurers, are poised for substantial gains from the upcoming National Stock Exchange IPO. SBI alone expects to rake in over ₹5,000 crore. Other funds and institutions, with stakes dating back to the 90s, are also set to receive significant returns.

business.title: US stocks: US market's indexes advance with boost from chips, Iran optimism
business.summary: U.S. stock indexes rose Thursday, led by semiconductor shares and easing inflation fears, despite anticipated Federal Reserve interest rate hikes. Intel surged to a record high after President Trump announced a deal for U.S.-based chip design and manufacturing. Oil prices slid as the U.S. and Iran extended an interim agreement, allowing for continued passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

business.summary: SpaceX is preparing to discuss a massive bond offering with investors. The company plans to raise at least $20 billion. This move follows its record initial public offering. The funds will refinance a significant bridge loan. This is SpaceX's first foray into issuing investment-grade US dollar bonds. The company is also embracing artificial intelligence.

Brent crude oil price touches pre-Iran war lows after ceasefire deal
business.summary: Oil prices have fallen significantly. This is due to a preliminary agreement between the United States and Iran. The deal aims to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and lift sanctions. This is expected to boost global oil supply. Analysts anticipate a gradual recovery in oil flows. Some experts believe prices may not plummet further.

business.title: Trump’s Iran deal extends ceasefire, opens Hormuz

business.title: Bernie Sanders pushes 50%t public stake in AI giants, $1,000 dividend payout to every American
business.summary: Senator Bernie Sanders has introduced legislation proposing a 50% public ownership stake in major US AI companies through a one-time stock tax. This plan aims to redistribute the industry's wealth, with an estimated $7 trillion fund potentially distributing over $1,000 annually to every American.

business.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Friday
business.summary: Indian equities ended higher for a fifth straight session, recovering early losses despite hawkish Federal Reserve comments. Banking stocks led gains, while broader markets stayed positive. Analysts remain optimistic on Nifty's trend but caution that near-term consolidation may follow the recent rally.

business.title: RBI to conduct Rs 1 lakh cr 3-day VRR auction on Jun 19
business.summary: The Reserve Bank of India will conduct a Rs 1 lakh crore variable rate repo auction on June 19. This move aims to manage liquidity in the banking system. The auction will help keep overnight money market rates stable. This follows recent liquidity infusions by the central bank. The RBI is actively managing funds to ensure smooth financial operations.

business.title: Sebi proposes easing margin trading funding rules, tighter broker norms

technology.title: Xiaomi Wants a Bigger Space in Your Home: Confirms Entry Into the Large Appliances Category in India
technology.summary: Xiaomi India has confirmed it will launch large appliances in the second half of 2026. The senior leadership team at Xiaomi India, while addressing a small section of the media, announced the news. At this point, there's no update on which product will launch first, but hinted that local testing is already on. The company also touched upon localising manufacturing, and this could mean that we may see Made-in-India large appliances with the Xiaomi logo in India before the end of 2026.

technology.title: Xiaomi India CMO Anuj Sharma Steps Down After 8 Years
technology.summary: Anuj Sharma, CMO, Xiaomi India, has stepped down from the role after nearly eight years. Talking to select media earlier this week, Anuj hinted that the decision was made quite some time ago, rather than being a sudden flashpoint. Sharma also joked about how the absolute pressure cooker of the Indian smartphone space over the last eight years has physically aged him.
